extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won with overwhelming sprint speed after a straight leadout, accelerating once and gapping the field in 100 meters.
tactical_patterns: VAN HOEK Harm's solo bridge attempt on the climb forced breakaway to accelerate prematurely, showing timed counterattacks drain breakaways.
Sprinter teams intentionally slowed peloton on climbs to avoid redlining their fastmen 60 miles from finish, prioritizing recovery.
JAKOBSEN required only 100m full-gas sprint when positioned third wheel, suggesting energy conservation matters more than early positioning.
TRENTIN Matteo jumped early from right side but JAKOBSEN's first pedal stroke created permanent gap, revealing acceleration beats positioning.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Won with such dominant first acceleration that positioning errors by rivals became irrelevant; consolidating points lead
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Good sprint but lacks speed to challenge JAKOBSEN, needs hillier sprint stages to win
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Commentary notes theoretical mathematical points jersey challenge via time trial and MTFs, revealing stage-hunting ambitions
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Crashed earlier in stage alongside MAS Enrique, showing vulnerability on nervous flat stages
